candidate_problems.text: What precise principle determines which components of quantum energy gravitate and which are gauge-, scheme-, or coarse-graining-dependent artifacts? The core unresolved tension is to replace the naive rule 'sum vacuum energies and couple to gravity' with a theory-level invariant map from microphysics to spacetime curvature that survives changes of cutoff, renormalization scheme, background geometry, and phase transitions.

Can a concrete mechanism—symmetry, sequestering, constraint, emergent gravity, or selection rule—suppress vacuum gravitation while remaining consistent with precision local QFT, semiclassical gravity, and cosmological observations? The open problem is not just conceptual but constructive: produce a model that explains the smallness and stability of dark energy without hidden fine-tuning or conflict with known physics.

What observational discriminants can separate a small residual geometric cosmological constant from dynamical or emergent dark-energy mechanisms that alter the gravitational response to vacuum structure? This tension matters because the conjecture predicts that different deeper structures should leave distinct signatures in time evolution, coupling to large-scale structure, or departures from standard gravity, yet it is unclear which measurements have decisive leverage.
